entities.name: PIGEON PEA

entities.description: An environmental stressor that pigeon pea is resistant to

entities.name: SALINITY
entities.description: An environmental stressor that pigeon pea is resistant to

entities.description: Nitrogen-fixing bacteria that nodulate pigeon pea plants

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The region, specifically central and southern areas, where the rhizobial isolates were obtained

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC TRAITS
entities.description: A set of 91 characteristics used to characterize bacterial isolates

entities.name: CULTURAL CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: Phenotypic traits related to the growth and appearance of bacterial cultures

entities.name: ANTIBIOTIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The intrinsic ability of rhizobial isolates to resist antibiotics

entities.name: HEAVY METAL RESISTANCE
entities.description: The intrinsic ability of rhizobial isolates to resist heavy metals

entities.name: SALT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rhizobial isolates to tolerate salt

entities.name: PH TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rhizobial isolates to tolerate different pH levels

entities.name: TEMPERATURE TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rhizobial isolates to tolerate various incubation temperatures

entities.name: CARBON SOURCE UTILIZATION
entities.description: The ability of rhizobial isolates to utilize various carbon sources

entities.name: NITROGEN SOURCE UTILIZATION
entities.description: The ability of rhizobial isolates to utilize various nitrogen sources

entities.name: SYMBIOTIC PROPERTIES
entities.description: Properties related to the symbiotic relationship between rhizobia and the host plant

entities.name: UPGMA
entities.description: Unweighted pair group method with arithmetic means used for comparing rhizobial strains

entities.name: NTSYSPC VERSION 2.1
entities.description: Software program used for the application of UPGMA and cluster analysis

entities.name: CLUSTER ANALYSIS
entities.description: A method used to group rhizobial isolates based on phenotypic traits

entities.name: BRADYRHIZOBIUM JAPONICUM
entities.description: A reference strain of rhizobia used for comparison

entities.name: BRADYRHIZOBIUM ELKANII
entities.description: A reference strain of rhizobia used for comparison

entities.name: SYMBIOTIC EFFECTIVENESS
entities.description: The ability of rhizobial isolates to effectively nodulate the host plant

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSES
entities.description: General environmental pressures that certain rhizobial strains can tolerate

entities.name: INOCULANT
entities.description: A product developed from effective rhizobial strains for plant inoculation
